cxu_object.claim: The momentum signal measures short-horizon directional projections from price velocity and volatility-adjusted continuation or reversion, using ROC and acceleration-based direction scoring, and is most reliable at the five-minute horizon where it captures genuine momentum before noise dominates

supporting_contexts.text: Momentum signal computes rate-of-change (ROC) across multiple windows and acceleration (second derivative of price). Direction score is derived from ROC direction weighted by acceleration — positive acceleration in the direction of ROC increases confidence. Volatility adjustment scales projections by current ATR to avoid overshooting in calm markets or undershooting in volatile ones.

Primary horizon: 5m. The 1m horizon is emitted only under strict data quality conditions. 15m serves as confirmation. 1h is conditional on trend context. Key metadata fields: rawConfidence (pre-capped confidence), dataQuality (ok/degraded/stale indicating data freshness). When dataQuality is degraded or stale, confidence should be discounted heavily.

primaryHorizon.description: Most reliable horizon for this signal type

trustLevel.description: Current trust level: low/medium/high based on observed accuracy

version.change_description: Initial signal type CxU from momentum signal spec
